2026-04-22T19:14:53.1242209Z ##[group]Run # Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1242713Z [36;1m# Generate a secure random API key (360 bits of entropy, 40+ chars)[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1243384Z [36;1m# Mask immediately to prevent timing vulnerabilities[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1243728Z [36;1mAPI_KEY=$(openssl rand -base64 45 | tr -d '/+=')[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244020Z [36;1mecho "::add-mask::${API_KEY}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244269Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244423Z [36;1mPORT=3001[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244601Z [36;1m[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244767Z [36;1m# Set outputs for next steps[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1244985Z [36;1m{[0m

2026-04-22T19:14:53.1245438Z [36;1m  echo "safe_outputs_port=${PORT}"[0m
2026-04-22T19:14:53.1245688Z [36;1m} >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"[0m

2026-04-22T19:17:36.0166786Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T19:15:47.932248208Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58514,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and AddAgentServerCore/UseAgentServerCore Tier 3 pattern in Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58396) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 added two significant new public APIs in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.23:\\n\\n1. **`PlatformHeaders` static class** — Centralizes all cross-protocol HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). This is a new **public** type visible in the API listing.\\n2. **`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` extension methods** — Replaced six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId`, `AddAgentServerVersion`, `AddAgentServerLogging`, and their `Use*` counterparts) with a single pair. This is documented as a breaking change in CHANGELOG.md, but the README has no code example showing this Tier 3 standalone setup pattern.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a public class in the API listing (`api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.net8.0.cs`) but is absent from the README\\u0026#39;s **Key concepts** section — developers building custom middleware or handlers have no guidance that these constants exist.\\n- The `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is only mentioned in a single sentence in the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84 of README); there is no code snippet showing how to wire up the Tier 3 standalone setup, making the breaking change from 6 methods to 2 invisible to new readers.\\n- The Getting Started section has no \\u0026#34;Standalone / Tier 3 setup\\u0026#34; sub-section showing `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` usage alongside the existing `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` (Tier 1/2) example.\\n- No sample test file backing a `PlatformHeaders` or Tier 3 snippet exists under `tests/Snippets/`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the \\u0026#34;Health endpoint\\u0026#34; subsection), add a new `### PlatformHeaders` subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\nA static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages. Refer to these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings in custom middleware or handlers:\\n\\n| Constant | Header | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request \\u0026amp; response — correlation ID |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response — SDK identity |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response — resolved session ID |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request — user partition key |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request — conversation partition key |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request — W3C trace context propagation |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request — Azure SDK correlation |\\n```\\n\\n2. In the **Getting started** section, add a new `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section **after** the existing `### Start a server (recommended)` sub-section. The new sub-section should explain when to use this pattern and include a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\\n\\nIf you are integrating AgentServer middleware into an existing ASP.NET Core application rather than using `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`, call `AddAgentServerCore()` during service registration and `UseAgentServerCore()` during middleware configuration:\\n\\n```\\n// In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);\\nbuilder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\nvar app = builder.Build();\\napp.UseAgentServerCore();\\n// ... map your protocol routes ...\\napp.Run();\\n```\\n\\nThis registers the `x-request-id` response header middleware, the server version (`x-platform-server`) header middleware, and inbound request logging — the same middleware that `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` enables automatically.\\n```\\n\\n3. Update the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84) to reference the new Getting Started sub-section instead of re-explaining the methods inline.\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new test method backing the `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` snippet:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Explicit(\\u0026#34;Snippets are compiled to prevent rot but require a running server to execute.\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic void Tier3StandaloneSetup()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\\n\\n    // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\\n    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args: []);\\n    builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\n    var app = builder.Build();\\n    app.UseAgentServerCore();\\n    // ... map your protocol routes ...\\n    app.Run();\\n\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAlso add the required `using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;` import if not already present.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Test file path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`\\n   - Add method `Tier3StandaloneSetup` with `#region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` / `#endregion`\\n   - Any test-only assertions must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\n2. **README placeholder:** In `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`, under the new \\u0026#34;### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\\u0026#34; sub-section, insert:\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n   The snippet name is `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` — must match exactly between the test region and the README placeholder.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already has the required top-level sections. The following updates are needed:\\n\\n- **Getting started** — Add `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section *(missing)*\\n- **Key concepts** — Add `### PlatformHeaders` sub-section *(missing)*\\n- All other required sections (Prerequisites, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the Tier 3 snippet\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public type changes expected; verify API listing is unchanged\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; - `centralus2.in.applicationinsights.azure.com`\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; To allow these domains, add them to the `network.allowed` list in your workflow frontmatter:\\n\\u0026gt;\\n\\u0026gt; ```yaml\\n\\u0026gt; 2026-04-22T19:17:36.0230778Z {"timestamp":"2026-04-22T19:15:48.119664014Z","direction":"IN","type":"RESPONSE","server_id":"github","payload":{"id":1,"jsonrpc":"2.0","result":{"content":[{"text":"{\"number\":58514,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document PlatformHeaders and AddAgentServerCore/UseAgentServerCore Tier 3 pattern in Core README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n**Triggered by:** commit [REDACTED] (PR #58396) by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58396 added two significant new public APIs in `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core` 1.0.0-beta.23:\\n\\n1. **`PlatformHeaders` static class** — Centralizes all cross-protocol HTTP header name constants (`x-request-id`, `x-platform-server`, `x-agent-session-id`, isolation keys, `traceparent`, `x-ms-client-request-id`). This is a new **public** type visible in the API listing.\\n2. **`AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` extension methods** — Replaced six individual extension methods (`AddAgentServerRequestId`, `AddAgentServerVersion`, `AddAgentServerLogging`, and their `Use*` counterparts) with a single pair. This is documented as a breaking change in CHANGELOG.md, but the README has no code example showing this Tier 3 standalone setup pattern.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- `PlatformHeaders` is a public class in the API listing (`api/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core.net8.0.cs`) but is absent from the README\\u0026#39;s **Key concepts** section — developers building custom middleware or handlers have no guidance that these constants exist.\\n- The `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` pair is only mentioned in a single sentence in the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84 of README); there is no code snippet showing how to wire up the Tier 3 standalone setup, making the breaking change from 6 methods to 2 invisible to new readers.\\n- The Getting Started section has no \\u0026#34;Standalone / Tier 3 setup\\u0026#34; sub-section showing `AddAgentServerCore()` / `UseAgentServerCore()` usage alongside the existing `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` (Tier 1/2) example.\\n- No sample test file backing a `PlatformHeaders` or Tier 3 snippet exists under `tests/Snippets/`.\\n\\n\\n\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`** — Edit\\n\\n1. In the **Key concepts** section (after the \\u0026#34;Health endpoint\\u0026#34; subsection), add a new `### PlatformHeaders` subsection:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### PlatformHeaders\\n\\nA static class that centralises all HTTP header name constants used across AgentServer packages. Refer to these constants instead of hard-coding header name strings in custom middleware or handlers:\\n\\n| Constant | Header | Direction |\\n|---|---|---|\\n| `PlatformHeaders.RequestId` | `x-request-id` | Request \\u0026amp; response — correlation ID |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ServerVersion` | `x-platform-server` | Response — SDK identity |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.SessionId` | `x-agent-session-id` | Response — resolved session ID |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.UserIsolationKey` | `x-agent-user-isolation-key` | Request — user partition key |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ChatIsolationKey` | `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` | Request — conversation partition key |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.TraceParent` | `traceparent` | Request — W3C trace context propagation |\\n| `PlatformHeaders.ClientRequestId` | `x-ms-client-request-id` | Request — Azure SDK correlation |\\n```\\n\\n2. In the **Getting started** section, add a new `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section **after** the existing `### Start a server (recommended)` sub-section. The new sub-section should explain when to use this pattern and include a snippet placeholder:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\\n\\nIf you are integrating AgentServer middleware into an existing ASP.NET Core application rather than using `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()`, call `AddAgentServerCore()` during service registration and `UseAgentServerCore()` during middleware configuration:\\n\\n```\\n// In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\\nvar builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args);\\nbuilder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\nvar app = builder.Build();\\napp.UseAgentServerCore();\\n// ... map your protocol routes ...\\napp.Run();\\n```\\n\\nThis registers the `x-request-id` response header middleware, the server version (`x-platform-server`) header middleware, and inbound request logging — the same middleware that `AgentHost.CreateBuilder()` enables automatically.\\n```\\n\\n3. Update the Troubleshooting/Logging section (line 84) to reference the new Getting Started sub-section instead of re-explaining the methods inline.\\n\\n**File: `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`** — Edit\\n\\nAdd a new test method backing the `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` snippet:\\n\\n```csharp\\n[Test]\\n[Explicit(\\u0026#34;Snippets are compiled to prevent rot but require a running server to execute.\\u0026#34;)]\\npublic void Tier3StandaloneSetup()\\n{\\n    #region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\\n\\n    // In Program.cs of an existing ASP.NET Core app:\\n    var builder = WebApplication.CreateBuilder(args: []);\\n    builder.Services.AddAgentServerCore();\\n\\n    var app = builder.Build();\\n    app.UseAgentServerCore();\\n    // ... map your protocol routes ...\\n    app.Run();\\n\\n    #endregion\\n}\\n```\\n\\nAlso add the required `using Microsoft.Extensions.DependencyInjection;` import if not already present.\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\n1. **Test file path:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/tests/Snippets/Sample1Snippets.cs`\\n   - Add method `Tier3StandaloneSetup` with `#region Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` / `#endregion`\\n   - Any test-only assertions must be wrapped in `#if !SNIPPET` / `#endif`\\n\\n2. **README placeholder:** In `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md`, under the new \\u0026#34;### Standalone (Tier 3) setup\\u0026#34; sub-section, insert:\\n   ````\\n   ```C# Snippet:Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone\\n   ```\\n   ````\\n   The snippet name is `Core_ReadMe_Tier3_Standalone` — must match exactly between the test region and the README placeholder.\\n\\n### Step 3: Verify README structure\\n\\nThe README at `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/README.md` already has the required top-level sections. The following updates are needed:\\n\\n- **Getting started** — Add `### Standalone (Tier 3) setup` sub-section *(missing)*\\n- **Key concepts** — Add `### PlatformHeaders` sub-section *(missing)*\\n- All other required sections (Prerequisites, Examples, Troubleshooting, Next steps, Contributing) are present.\\n\\nUse `sdk/keyvault/Azure.Security.KeyVault.Secrets/README.md` as the canonical reference for section formatting and tone.\\n\\n### Step 4: Validate\\n\\nRun these commands in order. Each must succeed before proceeding to the next:\\n\\n1. `dotnet build sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n2. `dotnet test sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/ --filter TestCategory!=Live`\\n3. `eng/scripts/Update-Snippets.ps1 agentserver` — confirm README was updated with the Tier 3 snippet\\n4. `eng/scripts/Export-API.ps1 agentserver` — no public type changes expected; verify API listing is unchanged\\n5. `dotnet format sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Core/`\\n\\n\\n\\n## Next Steps\\n\\n\\u0026gt; [!TIP]\\n\\u0026gt; **Ready for automated implementation?** Assign this issue to **`@copilot`** to have Copilot coding agent implement the changes described in the Implementation Guide above\\n\\n\\n\\n\\n\\u0026gt; :\"rogerbarreto\",\"id\":19890735,\"node_id\":\"MDQ6VXNlcjE5ODkwNzM1\",\"avatar_url\":\"https://avatars.githubusercontent.com/u/19890735?v=4\",\"html_url\":\"https://github.com/rogerbarreto\",\"gravatar_id\":\"\",\"type\":\"User\",\"site_admin\":false,\"url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto\",\"events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/events{/privacy}\",\"following_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/following{/other_user}\",\"followers_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/followers\",\"gists_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/gists{/gist_id}\",\"organizations_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/orgs\",\"received_events_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/received_events\",\"repos_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/repos\",\"starred_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/starred{/owner}{/repo}\",\"subscriptions_url\":\"https://api.github.com/users/rogerbarreto/subscriptions\"}],\"node_id\":\"I_kwDOACyxMM7--0UJ\"},{\"id\":4280857957,\"number\":58268,\"state\":\"open\",\"locked\":false,\"title\":\"[AgentServer] Docs: Document beta.2 features in Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses README\",\"body\":\"## Documentation Gap\\n\\n**Package:** `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`\\n**Service directory:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/`\\n**Triggered by:** PR #58252 and release commit cc01a6c by `@RaviPidaparthi`\\n\\n## What Changed\\n\\nPR #58252 added the following significant features to `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses`, all released in 1.0.0-beta.2:\\n\\n- **Chat isolation key enforcement**: new `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header that, when supplied on `CreateResponse`, must be repeated on all subsequent GET, Cancel, DELETE, and InputItems calls. Mismatches silently return 404 for security isolation.\\n- **`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy`**: a new Azure.Core per-retry pipeline policy that logs every outbound Foundry storage call with method, URI, status code, duration, and correlation headers (`x-ms-client-request-id`, `x-ms-request-id`, `x-request-id`, `apim-request-id`).\\n- **Metadata validation constraints**: metadata maps are now validated for ≤ 16 keys, key length ≤ 64 chars, value length ≤ 512 chars.\\n- **Malformed response ID validation**: both `response_id` path parameter and `previous_response_id` body field are validated for format; malformed values return 400.\\n- **Error response shape corrections**: cancel-after-delete now returns 404 (was 400); `invalid_request_error` type is now used consistently.\\n\\n## Gaps Found\\n\\n- The **Key concepts** section of the README has no mention of `x-agent-chat-isolation-key`. Users who need multi-tenant isolation have no guidance on how to use it or what the header enforcement contract is.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Logging** section says only \\\"The library emits OpenTelemetry traces via `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\\" It does not mention `FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` or how to enable logging of outbound Foundry storage calls for diagnostics.\\n- The **Troubleshooting / Common errors** section incorrectly says cancel returns `400 Bad Request` for terminal-state responses, and doesn't mention that cancel-after-delete returns **404**. It also doesn't mention the new **400** for malformed response IDs.\\n- No documentation exists for the metadata constraints (16-key, 64/512-char limits) that now produce validation errors.\\n\\n\\u003cdetails\\u003e\\n\\u003csummary\\u003e\\u003cstrong\\u003e📐 Implementation Guide\\u003c/strong\\u003e\\u003c/summary\\u003e\\n\\nThis section contains step-by-step instructions for a coding agent to implement the changes described above.\\n\\n### Step 1: Modify files\\n\\n**File:** `sdk/agentserver/Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses/README.md` — **edit**\\n\\n#### 1a. Add a \\\"Chat isolation\\\" subsection under Key concepts\\n\\nAfter the existing `### Thread safety` subsection, add:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Chat isolation\\n\\nThe `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` header enables multi-tenant isolation. When a response is\\ncreated with this header, all subsequent operations for that response — GET, Cancel, DELETE, and\\nInputItems — must supply the same key. A missing or mismatched key returns `404 Not Found`\\n(indistinguishable from a non-existent response) so that cross-tenant enumeration is not possible.\\n\\nWhen no isolation key is set at creation time, no enforcement is applied to subsequent calls.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1b. Update the Troubleshooting / Common errors section\\n\\nReplace the existing bullet list with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Common errors\\n\\n- **400 Bad Request**: The request body failed validation. Check that optional fields such as\\n  `model` (when provided) are valid, that `input` items are well-formed, that `metadata` contains\\n  ≤ 16 keys with key length ≤ 64 characters and value length ≤ 512 characters, and that any\\n  supplied `previous_response_id` matches the expected ID format.\\n- **400 Bad Request** (malformed ID): The `response_id` path parameter or the `previous_response_id`\\n  request body field does not match the expected format (prefix `caresp_` followed by 50 characters).\\n- **400 Bad Request** (cancel): The response has already reached a terminal state\\n  (`completed`, `failed`, or `cancelled`). The response body contains an\\n  `invalid_request_error` error type with the message `\\\"Cannot cancel a response in terminal state.\\\"`.\\n- **404 Not Found**: The response ID does not exist, has expired past the configured TTL, was\\n  created with `store=false`, or was created with an `x-agent-chat-isolation-key` that does not\\n  match the key provided in this request.\\n- **404 Not Found** (cancel or delete): Returned when the response does not exist *or* has\\n  already been deleted — cancel-after-delete returns 404 per spec.\\n```\\n\\n#### 1c. Update the Troubleshooting / Logging section\\n\\nReplace the existing paragraph under `### Logging` with:\\n\\n```markdown\\n### Logging\\n\\nThe library emits OpenTelemetry traces via the `Azure.AI.AgentServer.Responses` activity source.\\n\\n#### Foundry storage call logging\\n\\nWhen the Responses library is backed by Azure AI Foundry storage (`FoundryStorageProvider`),\\n`FoundryStorageLoggingPolicy` logs every outbound HTTP call at `Information` level on success and\\n`Warning`/`Error` level on failures. Each log entry includes:\\n\\n- HTTP method and URI\\n- HTTP status code and latency\\n- Correlation headers: `x-ms-client-request-id` (outbound), `x-ms-request-id`,\\n  `x-request-id`, and `apim-request-id` (inbound from Foundry)\\n\\nTo enable these logs, configure the Microsoft.Extensions.Logging `Information` level for\\n`Azure.AI.AgentServer` in your `appsettings.json`:\\n\\n```json\\n{\\n  \\\"Logging\\\": {\\n    \\\"LogLevel\\\": {\\n      \\\"Azure.AI.AgentServer\\\": \\\"Information\\\"\\n    }\\n  }\\n}\\n```\\n```\\n\\n### Step 2: Add or update code snippets\\n\\nNo new snippet test files are required for these changes — the documentation additions are\\nexplanatory prose, not code samples. However, if a sample demonstrating chat isolation is\\ndesired, add a new test file:\\n\\n**Path:** `sdk/agents
2026-04-22T19:17:36.0373530Z ...
